Petition For Order Approving Settlement, Appointment Of Successor Fiduciaries, And Modification Of Terms Of Trust To Confirm To Settlement Terms
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Advanced From Dec-18-18 To Nov-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement, Appointment Of Successor Fiduciaries, And Modification Of Terms Of Trust To Confirm To Settlement Terms
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Georgine Marrott.
Andrew Shultz for Scott Hodges (proposed Trustee).
Mark Marrott in Propria Persona.

Ms. Calhoun stated that they resolved their issues and that they have signed an amendment to the agreement. All Parties agree with Ms. Calhoun's statement. The Court stated that the only issue is that they are asking to modify the Trust, that it is duplicative, and that it does not think it is necessary or appropriate. Ms. Calhoun stated that it is in her petition and there is no opposition to it. The Court granted the petition as to approval of the settlement. The issue of modifying the trust is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement, Appointment Of Successor Fiduciaries, And Modification Of Terms Of Trust To Confirm To Settlement Terms
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Dec-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Georgine Marrott (Petitioner).
Mark Marrott in Propria Persona.

This was a pre-granted matter. Mr. Marrott stated that he is not objecting, but there are two things he has questions about. The Court informed Mr. Marrott that it can continue the matter if he wishes to object and put it in writing, and that it can not give him legal advise. Mr. Marrott stated that he is asking to stay in the house until it is sold. Ms. Calhoun stated that it appears Mr. Marrott wants to modify the agreement and that it is time sensitive. The Petition for Order Approving Settlement, etc. is continued to December 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Marrott is to file his objections no later than December 5, 2018. If Mr. Marrott is not going to object, the Court is to be notified by December 5th.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.

Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott'S Petition For Orders: 1) Declaring Mark Marrot To Be A Trustee De Son Tort And Requiring Him To: 1) Account Fo
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Continued To Sep-04-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott'S Petition For Orders: 1) Declaring Mark Marrot To Be A Trustee De Son Tort And Requiring Him To: 1) Account Fo
Off Calendar Jun-19-2018 Continued To Aug-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Georgine Marrott
John Kithas for and with Mark Marrott

The Court noted that Ms. Marrott has passed away, mediation is scheduled for late next month, and the house is not to be sold. Both Counsel reported that Ms. Hinshaw has no continuing role in this matter. The Court has Ms. Calhoun's proposed order with Mr. Kithas objections. Mr. Kithas submits a new proposed order in open court. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to subpoenas for production of documents and appointment of a Trustee / Co-Trustees. Mr. Kithas stated there is confusion whether the Will or Trust is earlier, stated that Mr. Marrott has located the key to the safe deposit box, and proposed that they go together to inventory what is in the safe deposit box. Mr. Marrott stated that the safe deposit box in his mother's name, both he and his sister have access to it, and the last time he went there was 2 1/2 - 3 years ago. The Court noted the pending petition may need to be amended depending on the outcome of the mediation next month. Ms. Calhoun stated that she is aware of the need to amend the petition.

The Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott's Petition for Order, etc. is continued to August 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. By August 14, 2018 parties to submit (if possible, a joint) status statement regarding results of mediation. The Court urges parties to cooperate with document production, whether/who temporary Trustee should be appointed, and for an inventory of the safe deposit box. The Court will review the proposed orders submitted by Counsel regarding protection of Trust Property.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reporter: Lana L. Loper CSR 667 (818)430-9151.

Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott'S Petition For Orders: 1) Declaring Mark Marrot To Be A Trustee De Son Tort And Requiring Him To: 1) Account Fo
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Continued To Jun-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Georgine Marrott (Petitioner)
John Kithas for Mark Marrott (Respondent)
Caroline Hinshaw for Kiki Marrott (proposed conservatee in related conservatorship matter) via CourtCall
Timothy Breen (proposed successor Trustee) present in court

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not to proceed with mediation, Kiki's capacity to handle her affairs, alleged undue influence, the need for a temporary Trustee, and access to estate planning documents and bank statement. Ms. Hinshaw stated that the fighting is not good for Kiki, that Kiki does not think her finances and estate planning are anyone's business, that she is prepared to disclose them, and that she cannot guarantee she has all of the estate planning documents. Ms. Hinshaw also stated that she cannot comment on financial arrangements made to cover Kiki's living expenses, and that she thinks the cash is not part of the Trust. The Court stated that it hopes the parties talk to each other in good faith, that Ms. Hinshaw can share what she can to keep the parties calm, and is prepared to order the Trust not dispose of assets, i.e. the house, absent an order of the court. The Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott's Petition for Orders, etc. is continued to June 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for mediation to have occurred and underlying disputes to be resolved. Ms. Calhoun is to submit a limited order, as to the house, with approval as to form.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott'S Petition For Orders: 1) Declaring Mark Marrot To Be A Trustee De Son Tort And Requiring Him To: 1) Account Fo
Off Calendar Apr-24-2018 Continued To May-22-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott'S Petition For Orders: 1) Declaring Mark Marrot To Be A Trustee De Son Tort And Requiring Him To: 1) Account Fo
Off Calendar Feb-13-2018 Continued To Apr-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Georgine Marrott (Petitioner).
John Kithas for Kiki Marrott and Mark Marrott (Respondents).

Mr. Kithas stated that a number of issues have been raised, that he has convinced Kiki to consider mediation and that he has made an offer in terms of a mental examination of Kiki. Ms. Calhoun stated that her Client's major concern is that the Brother Mark is influencing the Mother and that he is depleting the assets, and that her Client would like her Mother to have independent Counsel. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than April 13, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by April 18, 2018. The Petition for Orders Declaring Mark Marrott to Be a Trustee De Son Tort, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on April 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Reporter: Lori Nicora, CSR #6074, 415-362-4346.

Remainder Beneficiary Georgine Marrott'S Petition For Orders: 1) Declaring Mark Marrot To Be A Trustee De Son Tort And Requiring Him To: 1) Account Fo
Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Continued To Feb-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Georgine Marrott (Petitioner).
John Kithas for Bessie Marrott and Mark Marrott (Respondents).

Mr. Kithas stated that he e-filed a response yesterday and that they were unable to agree to a continuance. Ms. Calhoun expressed concern stating that Kiki is 103 years old, that she has been isolated from her family, that Kiki and Mark ("the wrong doer") are represented by the same Counsel, that Kiki did not go back to her original estate planning Attorney and instead engaged litigation Counsel. The Court raised the issue of mediation. Mr. Kithas stated that he would be happy to discuss mediation with his Clients. Ms. Calhoun suggested that they come back sooner rather than later with consideration of the Court appointing a Trustee Ad Litem. The Petition for Orders Declaring Mark Marrott to Be a Trustee De Son Tort, etc. is continued to February 13, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Reporter: Jennifer Roux, CSR #11033, 415-362-4346.
